Privacy.heading: Data Privacy
Privacy.description: Description of how Fractal manages, protects, and processes Client data, in accordance with the Federal Law on Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties (LFPDPPP).
Privacy.s1Text: The Client owns <bold>100% of their data and workflows</bold>. Fractal acts solely as a <bold>Data Processor</bold> under the LFPDPPP and acquires no rights over that information.
Privacy.s2Text: Fractal may use <bold>aggregated and anonymous</bold> statistics to improve the product. Under no circumstances are specific clients identified in such analyses, nor is individual data shared.
Privacy.s3Bullet1: Training its own models with client data.
Privacy.s3Bullet3: Sharing data with unauthorized third parties.
Privacy.s4Text: The Client authorizes the use of the following subprocessors upon contracting the service. Fractal will notify any changes with <bold>30 days' notice</bold>.
Privacy.s4Row2Value: AI models, per each workflow's configuration.
Privacy.s4Row3Value: AI models, per each workflow's configuration.
Privacy.s4Row4Value: AI models, per each workflow's configuration.
Privacy.s5Text: Data is stored in <bold>the United States (Google Cloud us-central)</bold>. International data transfers are carried out under the legal basis of <bold>Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC)</bold>, in accordance with the safeguards recognized by the LFPDPPP.
Privacy.s6Intro: Under the LFPDPPP, the Client has the right to:
Privacy.s6Bullet1: Access their data stored on the Platform.
Privacy.s6Bullet3: Cancel or delete their data (subject to the retention periods established in the Suspension Policy).
Privacy.s6Bullet4: Object to the processing of their data for specific purposes.
Privacy.s6Outro: To exercise any of these rights, contact us through the support panel clearly stating the request.
Privacy.finalNote: For any questions about the processing of your data, write to us at the privacy area through the support panel.

Terms.heading: Terms & Conditions
Terms.description: These Terms constitute the legal agreement between you and Fractal regarding access to and use of our AI-powered workflow automation platform.
Terms.s1Text1: These Terms and Conditions (the "Terms") constitute a legal agreement between you (the "Client") and Fractal regarding access to and use of our AI-powered workflow automation platform (the "Services").
Terms.s1Text2: By using the Services, you accept these Terms in full. If you disagree, do not use the Platform. We reserve the right to modify these Terms with <bold>30 days' prior notice</bold>.
Terms.s2Row2Value: Each workflow execution that consumes resources.
Terms.s2Row3Value: Contracted service level (Free, Starter, Growth, Business, Pro, Enterprise).
Terms.s2Row6Value: AI processing units (approximately 0.75 words per token).
Terms.s3Bullet1: Web and API access to the Platform.
Terms.s3Bullet3: Integration with AI models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google).
Terms.s3Bullet4: Management and monitoring panel, execution history.
Terms.s3Bullet5: Technical support according to the contracted Plan.
Terms.s3Bullet7: In-person training or data migration from other platforms.
Terms.s3Sub3Text: Fractal determines the most appropriate model for each request, selecting from the available models of its AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google). The Client cannot specify which model to use.
Terms.s4Text1: The system operates under a <bold>monthly credits</bold> model. Each Plan includes a credit balance that renews automatically at the start of each billing cycle. Unused credits carry over to the next month.
Terms.s4Text2: The cost structure is composed of the following elements:
Terms.s4Cost1Text: Recurring charge that includes the credit balance of the contracted Plan.
Terms.s4Cost2Text: When the monthly balance is exhausted, the system can continue operating in overage mode, subject to the Plan limit. Credits consumed in excess are billed at the end of the month at <bold>${price} USD per credit</bold>. The Free Plan does not allow overages.
Terms.s5Intro: Each workflow execution consumes credits according to its <bold>category</bold>, which determines complexity, token limit, and cost:
Terms.s5Row1Value: Simple, straightforward tasks · up to 20,000 tokens
Terms.s5Row2Value: Multi-step workflows with integrations · up to 100,000 tokens
Terms.s5Row3Value: Complex agents with advanced reasoning · up to 250,000 tokens
Terms.s5Outro: The category of each workflow is assigned by Fractal at creation time and reflects the required computational resources. An <bold>Advanced</bold> workflow with 5,000 monthly credits allows up to 250 executions; a <bold>Light</bold> one would allow up to 5,000 executions with the same balance.
Terms.s6Text1: The Client owns <bold>100% of their data and workflows</bold>. Fractal acts as Data Processor under the LFPDPPP.
Terms.s6Text2: Fractal is responsible when a workflow created by Fractal fails due to its own error: in that case it will correct it at no cost and compensate the Client with credits. Fractal is not responsible for failures by AI providers, the hosting provider, or force majeure events.
Terms.s6Text3: Fractal's total liability shall not exceed the <bold>amount paid by the Client in the last 12 months</bold>.
Terms.s7Text1: Fractal may modify features, pricing, or AI models with <bold>30 days' prior notice</bold>. During that period, the Client may cancel without penalty.
Terms.s7Text2: Fractal may suspend or terminate the service if the Client:
Terms.s7Bullet2: Engages in fraudulent or illegal activity.
Terms.s8Text: These Terms are governed by the laws of the <bold>United Mexican States</bold>. Any dispute shall be submitted to the jurisdiction of the courts of <bold>Aguascalientes, Mexico</bold>, expressly waiving any other jurisdiction that may apply.
Terms.s9Bullet1: These Terms constitute the entire agreement between the parties.
Terms.s9Bullet2: The Client may not assign this agreement without Fractal's written consent.
Terms.s9Bullet3: If any clause is found to be invalid, the remainder remains in force.
Terms.s9Bullet4: Failure to exercise a right does not constitute a waiver of that right.
Terms.finalNote: For inquiries about these Terms, contact us through our support panel.

Policies.heading: Policies
Policies.description: Cancellation policy, suspension for non-payment, service levels, technical support, and acceptable use of the Fractal Platform.
Policies.s1Text1: The Client may cancel their subscription at any time, without prior notice or penalties, as long as they are current on their payments. Upon cancellation, access to the Platform is lost <bold>immediately</bold>.
Policies.s1Text2: Payments made, including monthly subscriptions and the Setup Fee, <bold>are non-refundable under any circumstances</bold>.
Policies.s2Text: Upon cancelling a paid subscription, the Client is automatically downgraded to the <bold>Free Plan</bold>. The accumulated credit balance is preserved and can continue to be used to run workflows until exhausted.
Policies.s2Status1Desc: Full access to the Platform. Workflows can be executed with the available accumulated balance.
Policies.s2Status2Desc: Read access. The Client can view their data, history, and settings, but cannot run workflows until they top up or subscribe to a plan.
Policies.s2Status3Desc: Data is retained for an extended period. Fractal will notify the Client with sufficient advance notice before proceeding with any permanent deletion.
Policies.s2SubText: The Client can subscribe or reactivate a paid plan at any time. Their data, workflows, and history are fully preserved.
Policies.s3Bullet2: Human support: business hours 9 am–6 pm (Mexico time).
Policies.s3Bullet3: Languages: Spanish and English on all plans.
Policies.s3Row4Value: Email and WhatsApp · response within 12 hours
Policies.s3Row5Value: Email, WhatsApp and Account Manager · response within 12 hours
Policies.s3Row6Value: Email, WhatsApp and dedicated Account Manager · 4–8 hours
Policies.s4Bullet1: Illegal activity, including spam, phishing, fraud, and intellectual property violations.
Policies.s4Bullet2: Resource abuse: circumventing rate limits or sharing API Keys with third parties.
Policies.s4Bullet3: Including harmful or illegal content in workflows.
Policies.s4Bullet4: Use for mass disinformation or generating deepfakes without consent.
Policies.s4Bullet5: Unauthorized resale of the service (white label not permitted).
Policies.finalNote: To report a violation or inquire about these policies, contact us through the support panel.

SupportScreen.mainDesc: Find answers to frequently asked questions or contact us directly through our official channels.
SupportScreen.faq1Answer: Tesseract is an automation platform designed to empower your business. It lets you create, monitor, and manage automated workflows and executions, manage your team's internal access, and track resource consumption (credits) for your integrations, all in one place.
SupportScreen.faq2Question: How do I request a new Workflow?
SupportScreen.faq2Answer: From your dashboard, in the "Workflows" section, you can click "New Workflow". This will send a direct request to our team to schedule a meeting and define the technical details of your new custom automation.
SupportScreen.faq3Question: What are Credits and how are they consumed?
SupportScreen.faq3Answer: Credits are the unit of measurement for your workflow executions. Each successful or failed automated flow execution consumes credits according to its complexity level (Light, Standard, or Advanced). You can monitor this consumption in the "Billing" tab or your main Dashboard.
SupportScreen.faq4Question: Can I integrate Tesseract with my own systems?
SupportScreen.faq4Answer: Yes. Tesseract lets you securely generate "API Keys" from the corresponding tab. These keys allow you to run your workflows from your own web applications, internal systems, or via external API requests.

Integrations.deleteImpactTitle: Tesseract agents use connected integrations as the basis for executing their tasks. If an agent needs "{name}" and it is unavailable:
Integrations.deleteBullet1: Executions of associated workflows will end in error.
Integrations.deleteBullet2: If the integration is central to the agent, it will stop functioning entirely.
Integrations.deleteBullet3: Affected workflows will not recover on their own; they will require manual reconnection.
Integrations.deleteNote: Only proceed if you are sure this integration is not in use, or if you consciously accept that the workflows using it will stop working.
Integrations.disconnectWarning1: Disconnecting "{name}" will permanently delete its credentials. Any agent or workflow that depends on this integration will fail immediately when trying to use it.
